steps.title: Match corresponding angles
steps.content: Because the two triangles are similar, corresponding angles are congruent. The order of the triangle names shows that the angle at A in the first triangle corresponds to the angle at B in the second triangle.

steps.title: Use the given angle measure
steps.content: The side-length scale factor does not change angle measures. The corresponding angle at B is $59^{\circ}$, so the requested angle at A is also $59^{\circ}$. The answer is C.
